'''Shallow March''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] situated in the [[Mortex sector/Legends|Mortex sector]]. It hosted the [[Shallow March Supply Post]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], with the facility being under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Tarn Innis/Legends|Tarn Innis]] beginning around [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]]. At one point, Innis, who supplied military units of both the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], fended off the planet's [[Diet/Legends|predators]] via the use of [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] while providing supplies for the Alliance [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]].

==Description==
Shallow March<ref name="Fry">{{Twitter|jasoncfry|status/294566730532024320|[[Jason Fry]]|archivefile=File:JasonFryAtlas.png}}</ref> was a backwater [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CRO" /> located in the [[Shallow March system/Legends|Shallow March system]], a part of the [[Mortex sector/Legends|Mortex sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of<ref name="TransHydian" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Its fauna included a type of [[Diet/Legends|predator]].<ref name="CRO" />

==History==
[[File:Tarn_Innis.png|thumb|right|250px|Shallow March was the site of a Galactic Empire supply post under the command of Major Tarn Innis.]]
The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Supply Branch]] maintained the [[Shallow March Supply Post]] at the planet.<ref name="CRO" /> Around [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]],<ref name="4 BBY">''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', which is set shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], establishes that [[Tarn Innis/Legends|Tarn Innis]] was given [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of the [[Shallow March Supply Post]] four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, corresponding to approximately [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of the facility was given to the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] [[Major/Legends|major]] [[Tarn Innis/Legends|Tarn Innis]], who retained the post for the subsequent four [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Innis supplied any military units passing through the area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] Shallow March was located in, and he also provided supplies and parts to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and Rebel-affiliated [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]].<ref name="CRO" />

At one point, Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] visited the Shallow March Supply Post, where Innis's staff loaded gear needed by Cracken's unit. The Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officer]] conversed with Innis, with the Imperial [[Quartermaster/Legends|supply officer]] using [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] to fend off some of the planet's local predators that had approached the two. Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Cracken noted Innis's service on Shallow March in an entry dedicated to the Imperial that was part of a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet that hosted the Shallow March Supply Post was created by [[Sean Patrick Fannon]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Shallow March [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet, in grid square S-5.<ref name="TEA" /> [[Jason Fry]], co-author of ''The Essential Atlas'', has [[:File:JasonFryAtlas.png|stated]] that the star systems listed in the book's Appendix section are named after the most prominent planets or celestial bodies located in those systems. This article therefore considers the planet to be named in accordance with the Shallow March system.<ref name="Fry" />
